violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety certified with RIDOH. Application provided. PIC RICFSM expired in November 2024. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Some of the shellfish tags were lacking the date when the shellfish was last used. The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Storage document provided. Food relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was placed hot in the walk-in refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Pasta was cooled starting less than 2 hours ago. PIC unwrapped containers and placed in hotel pan to rapidly cool. Guidance document provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

A knife was observed stored between the prep units. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The floor in the excess storage area has a light accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. PIC stated all equipment will be pulled from walls and thoroughly cleaned. Pest control will be informed as preventative measure. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of bio-film. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Ice machine to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized prior to use. Inspector recommends increasing cleaning frequency. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Various foods were observed held between 42-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved all foods to walk-in. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Screen door is in poor repair. Screen must be replaced. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

The prep unit closest to the wall was observed with pooling water. When prep unit was in use, ambient was observed at 44 degrees. Food in unit was 42-44 degrees. PIC took unit out of service and called a technician to service unit. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
